sourceMovie.title: Moon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Moon for the same reason it broke through in 2009: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Duncan Jones keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The science fiction and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Martian
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Moon clicked for you, The Martian (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Moon clicked for you, Logan (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Moon?
faq.answer: Try The Martian, Logan, Cloud Atlas—each shares DNA with Moon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Moon?
faq.answer: Moon (2009) is best described as Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Moon part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Moon Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
